[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Mar 18, 2008 9:45 am On Werewolf Asuka- Asuka was meant to be a werewolf early on, but the designers fortunately got rid of that idea (And a whole lot of other crazy ones like Cat-Kaworu). I don't recall if Asuka was mentioned to be a "werewolf" in the Proposal, or even earlier than that. Majlund is right about a Markov process being run. Each point of NAveryW's um..."insight" has no memory of the previous point. @majlund- When exactly is a Markov process important in understanding computing systems? I had to look it up in Wikipedia, and I find it very interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Tue Mar 18, 2008 11:08 am @UrsusArctos I haven't really found Markov chains useful for anything beyond generating converting gibberish for fun and the Internet does it far better anyway. It could have its uses in error correcting codes (or information theory in general), but finite field arithmetics, basic probability and linear algebra would be a better investment of time.
